svg.text: UR Subnet — Mechanism at a glance

Two miner tiers in ONE mechanism (41% miner emission split by θ: routable-IP-breadth HEAD / implied_usage x Qn TAIL) — and every deposit is CONVICTION STAKE: locked in a buyback reserve, never distributed. §1, §7.4, §8.4–8.5.
holds NO α only — never holds
carry ingress / egress traffic inside a NO's pool — NOT on-chain UIDs
(subnet cap ≈ 256), so each is a client_id paid by Merkle claim.
claim α directly from the contract with an O(log N) proof.
a provider STARTS here (the low-barrier on-ramp / baseline).
standard 18 / 41 / 41 split — we do not fight the coinbase
validator early; the reserve backs it.
anyone who stakes own α, runs
across the two tiers of UIDs:
the top ~200 providers — each claims its OWN miner UID: the canonical
Bittensor treatment, MORE trust-minimized than the pool (no operator in path).
NATIVE emission straight to the provider's own hotkey each tempo —
no contract custody · no Merkle claim · no NO take, not shared.
identity: client_id <-> hotkey binding (§11.4) — dual-signed, fail-closed.
— split-adjusted routable egress IPs, NO deposit term (D27)
amount into the reserve. NO DT ledger:
cumulative locked α sets the NO's rate tier
emission ever touches a NO's keys.
validator weight w_n = implied_usage x Qn
NO commits a payout root each epoch;
it directs the split but never holds α.
every deposit, in full — staked to the
paid directly — no NO middleman, trust-minimized
a provider starts in a pool, GRADUATES to a top slot, and FALLS BACK if quality slips
pool = implied_usage x quality (baseline) · head = routable-IP breadth, native (merit apex) · both tiers paid from EMISSION ONLY
deposits are conviction stake (read from events, no DT ledger); validators weight the pools; each is a buy-and-lock buyback (§7.4); θ ≈ 0.3 then widen (§8.5)
